overview: The system uses a backend Go service exposing a REST API for leave management operations (submit requests, approve/deny, view balances, team calendar) and a React-based frontend web application that employees and managers interact with. The frontend calls the backend service for all data operations, and the backend maintains leave balances, requests, and user-manager relationships in memory or a simple data store.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests specifying start date, end date, and leave type
Managers must be able to view leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or deny leave requests
All users must be able to view their remaining leave balance by leave type
Employees must be able to view the status of their submitted leave requests
Managers must be able to view upcoming approved leave for their team on a calendar view
System must distinguish between employee and manager roles
System must track user-manager relationships to determine direct reports
System must support multiple leave types (e.g. annual, sick, personal)
All components must expose a /health endpoint for readiness checks

components.name: leave-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend API for employee leave requests and manager approvals
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/users/me:
    get:
      summary: Get current user profile
      responses:
        '200':
          description: User profile
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'
  /api/users/me/balance:
    get:
      summary: Get current user's leave balance
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveBalance'
  /api/leave-requests:
    get:
      summary: Get leave requests (for employees shows their own, for managers shows direct reports)
      parameters:
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, denied]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
    post:
      summary: Submit a new leave request
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequestInput'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request data
  /api/leave-requests/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get a specific leave request
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/leave-requests/{id}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request (manager only)
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Not authorized to approve this request
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/leave-requests/{id}/deny:
    post:
      summary: Deny a leave request (manager only)
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request denied
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Not authorized to deny this request
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/team/calendar:
    get:
      summary: Get team calendar of approved leave (manager only)
      parameters:
        - name: startDate
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date
        - name: endDate
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            format: date
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Approved leave entries for team members
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/CalendarEntry'
        '403':
          description: Not authorized (not a manager)
components:
  schemas:
    User:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string
          enum: [employee, manager]
        managerId:
          type: string
          nullable: true
    LeaveBalance:
      type: object
      properties:
        leaveType:
          type: string
        remaining:
          type: number
        total:
          type: number
    LeaveRequestInput:
      type: object
      required:
        - startDate
        - endDate
        - leaveType
      properties:
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        leaveType:
          type: string
        notes:
          type: string
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        employeeName:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        leaveType:
          type: string
        notes:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, denied]
        denialReason:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        reviewedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          nullable: true
        reviewedBy:
          type: string
          nullable: true
    CalendarEntry:
      type: object
      properties:
        employeeId:
          type: string
        employeeName:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        leaveType:
          type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go HTTP service using net/http listening on port 9090. The service manages leave requests for WSO2 employees. Use in-memory data structures (maps and slices) to store users, leave requests, and leave balances. Seed initial data with at least 5 users (mix of employees and managers) with manager-employee relationships, default leave balances (e.g. 20 annual, 10 sick, 5 personal days per user), and a few sample leave requests in various states. Implement all endpoints from the OpenAPI spec. For authentication, accept a user ID via X-User-ID header and look up the user in memory (simulate authentication). Validate that managers can only approve/deny requests from their direct reports (check managerId field). When a request is approved, deduct days from the employee's leave balance. Validate that employees have sufficient balance before creating requests. Validate date ranges (end date must be after start date, no past dates). Return appropriate HTTP status codes (200, 201, 400, 403, 404). The /health endpoint must return 200 with status ok. Use structured logging to stdout. Handle edge cases: overlapping leave requests for the same employee, requests spanning weekends, invalid leave types, attempting to approve already-reviewed requests. Generate unique IDs for leave requests using a counter or UUID approach. Ensure JSON responses match the schema exactly.

components.name: leave-ui
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management UI
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend web application for leave management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a React single-page application using TypeScript, React Router, and a component library like Material-UI or Ant Design. The app runs on port 8080 and serves static assets via a simple Node.js express server. Implement the following routes: / (dashboard showing user's leave balance and recent requests), /request (form to submit a new leave request with date pickers and leave type dropdown), /requests (list of user's own requests with status badges), /team (manager-only view showing pending requests from direct reports with approve/deny buttons), /calendar (manager-only calendar view of team's approved leave). Call the leave-api backend at http://leave-api:9090/api/* for all data operations. For authentication, hardcode the X-User-ID header to a sample user ID (e.g. user-1 for employee, user-3 for manager) or provide a simple dropdown to switch users for demo purposes. Display appropriate UI for employee vs manager role (hide manager routes for employees). Show loading states during API calls, error messages on failures, and success notifications on actions. Validate form inputs client-side before submitting. Use a calendar library like FullCalendar or react-big-calendar for the team calendar view. Implement responsive design for mobile and desktop. The /health endpoint must return 200 with status ok. Dockerize the app with a multi-stage build: install dependencies, build the React app, then serve with a lightweight Node server. Handle CORS if needed by proxying API requests through the Node server or configuring the backend. Show pending request count badge for managers. Allow filtering requests by status. Display leave balance prominently on dashboard. Edge cases: handle empty states (no requests, no team members), network errors, unauthorized access attempts, invalid date ranges in forms.
